weapons.name: Ohka Model 11
weapons.notes: The Ohka ('Cherry Blossom') was a rocket-powered piloted flying bomb, carried under a Mitsubishi G4M bomber and released near the target. Allied sailors called it 'Baka' (fool). Sank USS Mannert L. Abele in April 1945.

weapons.name: Type 93 Long Lance Torpedo
weapons.notes: The finest torpedo of the war. Oxygen-fuelled (leaving no bubble trail), it had four times the range of contemporary US torpedoes. Gave Japanese destroyers a decisive night-combat advantage in the Guadalcanal campaign.

weapons.name: Kaiten Type 1
weapons.notes: A manned suicide torpedo, essentially a modified Type 93 with a cockpit welded on. The pilot could not escape after launch. Deployed from submarines late 1944–1945; sank USS Mississinewa.
